programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Two sisters from Glasgow compete on the game show which sees contestants answer questions combining knowledge, strategy and luck to win life-changing prizes. After recently losing their grandfather, they would both like to use any winnings to treat their families. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Service for Palm Sunday led by Rev David Cameron of New Laigh Kirk, Kilmarnock and Rev Gillian Patterson of Wellsley Church in Methil. Together they ask how large crowds could gather to hail the entry of Jesus into Jerusalem then just days later be calling for his crucifixion. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley introduces a service for Palm Sunday from All Saints Church in Hale Barns. Sarah Small reflects on what Palm Sunday might mean today

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebutt hosts a special live celebration of the Jewish festival of Passover. He is joined by special guests including Rob Rinder and Rachel Stevens sharing their Passover traditions. There's great recipes from chefs Sarit Packer and Itamar Srulovich, and Helen McGinn will wake us all up to Kosher wines with her expert drink matches. Plus, great moments from the BBC food archive including The Hairy Bikers' visit to Israel

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Martin from London tries to get rid of a piece of furniture that holds a hidden secret

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: On Palm Sunday, Aled Jones visits Oxford to celebrate stories of hope, meeting a scientist behind the Covid-19 vaccine. YolanDa Brown meets musicians helping exhausted doctors

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Rudolph Walker appeals on behalf of the Stroke Association, a charity working to help people rebuild their lives after stroke

programme.title: My Mum Tracy Beaker
programme.desc: Jess and her mother move in with a famous footballer, but the lifestyle of a millionaire has an impact on the Beaker family. Feature-length episode of the children's drama

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley discovers how science is helping better understand nature on Bedfordshire's Greensand Ridge, where he reports on the world's longest running bird survey, helps to map the levels of plastic pollution, and joins the Toad Patrol creating a new national amphibian DNA database. Tom Heap investigates the UK's environmental ambitions, and Adam Henson provides a spring update on his farm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Christchurch Park in Ipswich, Suffolk, where treasures include an Imperial Chinese porcelain vase fit for an emperor and some intriguing portraits related to the Profumo scandal of the 1960s. Also featured are intricate, solid gold lapel badges from the Australian gold fields and a pair of delicate paintings by a famous Suffolk artist

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Having opened an official inquiry into DCI Jo Davidson and Operation Lighthouse, AC-12 begin to suspect a cover up. Steve and Chloe dig into Gail Vella's controversial reporting for clues to her murder, and find links to previous AC-12 cases. Their investigation takes a shocking turn when they begin to scrutinise Jo's personal life

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Israel v Scotland. Action from the qualifier at Bloomfield Stadium in Tel Aviv, as Steve Clarke's side continued their quest to make Qatar 2022

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of award-winning novelist Kazuo Ishiguro, who discusses his early life in Nagasaki and howe growing up in the home counties in the 1960s shaped his viewpoint as a writer. He also discusses his youthful desire to be a singer-songwriter, and chats about the music that has had a particular impact on his work. With contributions from writers Hanya Yanagihara and Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v West Ham United, Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal and Chelsea v Aston Villa

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Wimbledon to meet a pair of young developers with big plans for a two-bed house. He also visits a three-bed semi in Birmingham. Dion Dublin is in Bootle, Merseyside, to cast his eye over a two-bed semi-detached house

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics respond to a major incident on the A90 near Dundee. A crash between a van and a bus has led to multiple casualties, including one patient thrown from the top deck of the bus. Team Leader Mark must prioritise who needs the most urgent care as he works with dozens of other emergency responders at the scene of the crash

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Here, artists travel to the Wirral to create artworks to complement a recently completed house renovation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple on their country house hunt in Wiltshire. They have a generous £1million budget to afford them space for their prized possessions - a baby grand piano and classic cars. Away from the property search, Nicki discovers more about the art of wooden hat block making, a critically endangered heritage craft being kept alive by a Wiltshire-based family whose blocks are used by milliners across the world

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts, as London-based sisters Kavita and Sangeeta compete. The siblings both have busy lives and would love to get some new ideas for quick tasty meals they can make when they get home after a long day. They are aided in the kitchen by chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Art conservator Lucia Scalisi revives an oil painting of a mining scene, created by an amateur artist in the 1960s. Embroidery expert Sara Dennis and silversmith Brenton West join forces on a delicate wedding tiara seeking attention, while Steve Fletcher takes receipt of an early 20th-century chiming mantlepiece clock that was gifted to a couple on their wedding day. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Scotland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the final match of this year's tournament, taking place at Stade de France, as the home side looked to snatch this year's title from Wales. A winning margin of 21 points or more in a bonus-point win would be enough for France to reach the top of the table. Les Blues are looking for their first Six Nations win since 2010, when they also won the Grand Slam. The Scots also have plenty to play for in this fixture, as they look to end on a high and overtake Ireland into second place in the table.

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Guests Joanne McNally and Jimeoin join regulars Neil Delamere and Colin Murphy as they raise a socially distanced laugh or two in response to current events. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Liam Neeson and Micheal Richardson, who are playing father and son in comedy-drama Made in Italy. Breakout star of The Serpent, Tahar Rahim, talks about his gripping new legal drama The Mauritanian, while Cush Jumbo reflects on five-part thriller The Beast Must Die, in which he stars. Comedian and actor Rob Brydon and the undisputed queen of Dragons' Den Deborah Meaden also feature. With music by singer-songwriter Laura Mvula

programme.title: La La Land (2016)
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend

programme.desc: Nisha Katona heads to Yorkshire to discover authentic family recipes from Kashmir in Northern Pakistan, starting with a hearty Kashmiri breakfast at the Cafe Regal, where she also meets expert in Kashmiri food, Yazi. programme.title: World Questions
programme.desc: Understanding the options and decisions of scientists and politicians has never been more important, or more difficult. So how should we get the best information and how best can journalists and the public use this to question and hold the decision makers to account? Also, what's the best way to deal with 'fake news' - is it a major force or a distraction from the crisis? And what's the best way to counter to it? The BBC's Media Editor, Amol Rajan, will be joined by four leading experts from around the world and by members of the public who will put their questions to them

programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to Edinburgh to meet local nursery school pupils and read An Ceapaire Sgreamhail written by Gareth Edwards and illustrated by Hannah Shaw, and translated into Gaelic by Norma MacLeod

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)

programme.desc: The long-serving mailman sets off in the SDS plane to pick up a new sorting machine for the office

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo and her mum are playing hide and seek

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Dolina MacLennan reads Cion Fhaclan, written and illustrated by Natalie Russell and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Cameras follow Orinoco the osprey as he builds his strength up for his first epic migration. Starting in Canada, Orinoco faces treacherous weather, getting lost and starvation on his journey to South America, relying on his fishing technique and built-in navigational abilities to get him to the fish-filled waters of Colombia

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Crùbag is preparing fondue today

programme.desc: Seumas from Harris talks about his favourite hobbies, including piping and fishing

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Nobody understands where the Mùman family have gone or when they are returning, but they try their best to be welcoming when a visitor turns up at the Mùman house

programme.title: Fraochy Bay

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Winter sports, including ski jumps, bob sleds and motor skiing

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: Donald Morrison introduces vintage news reports from the Highlands and islands, including a portrayal of the activities of the Barra lifeboat and highlighting how work on the Tràigh Mhòr disrupted planes landing on Barra, and how the Eriskay Co-operative was reviving that island

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar travels to Point in Lewis, where she meets Maggie Mackenzie, known to many as the actress who played Fiona Macaskill in the long-running soap Machair

programme.title: About Britain: Isle of Skye
programme.desc: In 1954, Richard Dimbleby and a BBC film crew visited the Isle of Skye to record an episode of the early British television series About Britain. Here, he offers those viewing across the United Kingdom an insight into life on the island, meeting local characters and gaining an understanding of the lifestyle and culture of islanders

programme.title: Port

programme.title: The Flatey Enigma
programme.desc: Johanna recognises her ex Brynjar, and tries to keep Snorri a secret from him. In Icelandic

programme.desc: Music from acclaimed band Skerryvore, performing Take My Hand at the Trad Awards

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: A selection of tracks from the world of trad and folk music, including Eddi Reader and Ewen Henderson

programme.title: Òran na Mara (Song of the Sea) (2014)
programme.desc: Feature-length adventure. Ben and Saoirse set out to return to the Island where they grew up, a journey that pits them against mythical creatures and sees them confront the truth about who they really are

programme.title: Look Stranger
programme.desc: Two archive programmes looking at life on the Inner Hebridean islands of Gigha and Rum, originally broadcast in 1971. Local resident Donald Macdonald explains how the Gigha has survived the threat of depopulation and become a place of beauty and prosperity, and observe how Rhum was faring having been turned into Britain's largest conservation area at the time

programme.title: Pìobairean Bhòrnais (Pipers of Bornish)

programme.title: Gaisgich Oga an Darna Cogaidh
programme.desc: Calum does not care about the war and refuses to practise boring air raid drills. But while he is on his bike, German bombers fly over the town of Clydebank

programme.desc: Drama revolving around a high-end espresso machine. Ealasaid loaned her machine to Ailig's girlfriend Una, and desperately wants it back. Unfortunately, Ailig and Una just broke up

programme.desc: Keng breaks his friend’s computer game and does not deal very well with it. Instead of apologising to Joi, he ignores him - but Keng's dad tells him that he has to tell the truth

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Angela Maclean reports on the effect of the pandemic on refugees and asylum seekers in Glasgow, and a visit to Orkney and the world-leader in tidal power; Nigg Yard

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara/Mermaid Tales
programme.desc: A glimpse of another world full of wildlife and impressive scenery on the west coast of the Isle of Lewis, from the perspective of a young woman

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Day three of the Scottish National Sheep Dog Trial in Brisbane Glen Estate, West Kilbride, sees the competition intensify. Presenters Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee follow the action and also catch up with young handler Murray Common at his home in Lockerbie

programme.title: Lorraine

programme.title: This Morning

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Criminal barrister Robert Rinder takes on the cases of a brother and sister arguing over rent and two former friends in a dispute over a loan

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of family and friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, They then hen try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ian, Trudy, Chris and Arron pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs Wicket gets a new lodger who happens to be an inventor, so Bean foolishly tries to outdo him in the ingenuity stakes. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Louise snaps a photo of Mike while they play a game together. The only problem is that Mike looks absolutely ridiculous in it

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Katana continues to cause chaos around the city, and nobody can figure out who's side she is on

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Animated series based on the DC Comics superhero team the Teen Titans. Having gained the ability to transform into cars, the Titans continue to work together to fight Dr Military

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is thrilled when he wins an electronic home help device called Barry - finally, his family can boss something around other than him, but it is not long before Barry is overwhelmed by the Spuds' demands. Fighting back, Barry takes over Grimsby Heights and heads off to the sea for some well-earned peace and quiet.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker's favourite river walk finds her in Devon, following the green tree canopy of water rapids along the East Lyn and Hoar Oak rivers, where they merge at Watersmeet. While in the county, Julia makes sure she finds time to enjoy a hard-earned cream tea

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes Birmingham Royal Ballet director Carlos Acosta and singer and TV presenter JB Gill, while there is also look at the art of whittling, and some fun with rum

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The most memorable red and golden buzzer moments from the past 14 years of the auditions on the talent search, including the judges' reactions tp 100 Voices of Gospel, Kojo Amin and Paddy and Nico

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Albania v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from both teams' second 2022 FIFA World Cup Group I qualifier, held at Arena Kombetare in Tirana. The sides last met in the qualifying campaign for the 2002 World Cup, when England prevailed 3-1 in this city and 2-0 at Wembley Stadium. Gareth Southgate's men will expect to triumph once once this evening, given Albania have failed to follow up an historic qualification for Euro 2016 with further success since. With analysis by Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Ashley Cole, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Anglers flock to the village of Solomon Gorge desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to lurk in the lake. Their peace is ruined however when hundreds of sporting enthusiasts descend on the area for the Psycho Mud Run. Following the bitterness, fishing hooks and barbs are discovered hidden within the race obstacles before someone is found fatally electrocuted. Barnaby is left working out whether his death was another part of a sabotage gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the matchday two fixtures, including Albania v England at Arena Kombetare, Israel v Scotland at Bloomfield Stadium and Romania v Germany at Arena Nationala. Plus, highlights of the friendly between Northern Ireland and USA. Analysis comes from Lee Dixon, Ally McCoist and Ian Wright, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon, Joe Speight, Seb Hutchinson and Tom Gayle

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at how fields of yellow rapeseed flowers are turned into a kitchen cupboard staple, and a man who turned his passion for snowboarding into a company creating custom boards. Plus, the Bolton trainer factory with a racing pedigree, and how a Liverpudlian leather dynasty is producing traditional satchels that are sent around the world

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil action from Brands Hatch, featuring round 19 of the Ginetta GT4 Championship and round 26 of the British F4 Championship

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.desc: Alan welcomes Birmingham Royal Ballet director Carlos Acosta and singer and TV presenter JB Gill, while there is also look at the art of whittling, and some fun with rum

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The most memorable red and golden buzzer moments from the past 14 years of the auditions on the talent search, including the judges' reactions tp 100 Voices of Gospel, Kojo Amin and Paddy and Nico

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Albania v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from both teams' second 2022 FIFA World Cup Group I qualifier, held at Arena Kombetare in Tirana. The sides last met in the qualifying campaign for the 2002 World Cup, when England prevailed 3-1 in this city and 2-0 at Wembley Stadium. Gareth Southgate's men will expect to triumph once once this evening, given Albania have failed to follow up an historic qualification for Euro 2016 with further success since. With analysis by Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Ashley Cole, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Anglers flock to the village of Solomon Gorge desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to lurk in the lake. Their peace is ruined however when hundreds of sporting enthusiasts descend on the area for the Psycho Mud Run. Following the bitterness, fishing hooks and barbs are discovered hidden within the race obstacles before someone is found fatally electrocuted. Barnaby is left working out whether his death was another part of a sabotage gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the matchday two fixtures, including Albania v England at Arena Kombetare, Israel v Scotland at Bloomfield Stadium and Romania v Germany at Arena Nationala. Plus, highlights of the friendly between Northern Ireland and USA. Analysis comes from Lee Dixon, Ally McCoist and Ian Wright, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon, Joe Speight, Seb Hutchinson and Tom Gayle

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at how fields of yellow rapeseed flowers are turned into a kitchen cupboard staple, and a man who turned his passion for snowboarding into a company creating custom boards. Plus, the Bolton trainer factory with a racing pedigree, and how a Liverpudlian leather dynasty is producing traditional satchels that are sent around the world

programme.desc: Richard John Neil action from Brands Hatch, featuring round 19 of the Ginetta GT4 Championship and round 26 of the British F4 Championship

However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is delighted by the arrival of his older brother Sam, but their reunion turns to misery when Martin delivers bad news to one of them. Starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al tries to raise some quick cash, and offers to sort out all Louisa's household and plumbing problems if she can pay him promptly. The teacher gets good news on the employment front, and Martin accidentally inflicts a minor wound on an elderly patient - who responds by walking out of the surgery and vowing never to return. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Phyllida Law and Benjamin Whitrow

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin fail to find a lead when a 13-year-old is kicked to death at Euston Station, so they turn to Chandler, who holds a press conference. As the detectives are led through a tangled web of family loyalties, the suspect's barrister, an old flame of Steel's, gathers evidence for an audacious defence strategy that could change the nature of the British legal system. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike enter the junk market and use the garden to store their wares - until Sid puts his foot down. Comedy, starring Sally Geeson and Robin Stewart

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan buys a home brew kit and begins making his own beer - but underestimates its strength and turns up for work in no fit state to drive a bus. Comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Paul is panicked, Liv is suspicious, and Will does his best to distract Kim

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Growing up, Barbara Jacobs always believed that she was one of three children. It was not until she reached her twenties that she made a discovery which would change her life. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate Barbara's story, and also Sharon Hearte's. She was mixed race child adopted by a white family who grew up in the West Midlands. Sharon knew her birth mother, Barbara, was a white woman who worked in a Smethwick bicycle factory, and her father was from Jamaica. She wants to track down her mother to tell her that she understands why she was given up for adoption

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley travels back to the country of her birth, travelling the length and breadth of India to explore its diverse landscapes and cultural traditions. In this episode, her epic journey begins in Tamil Nadu, where she witnesses religious ceremonies in glorious temples, before visiting Gangtok in Sikkim, the place where her mother lived as a child

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's tries to join the pop scene make him a laughing stock and he realises the generation gap is wider than ever

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack persuade Inspector Blake to buy Arthur's old motorbike - but he runs into trouble on the test drive. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: One of the firm's clippies gets evicted leaving Stan feeling guilty as he was late getting her home, so he persuades Mum to take her in as a lodger. However, he soon regrets making the offer after she arrives accompanied by a large dog. Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney with Patricia Clapton

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates a break-in at the surgery, while Kate discovers the cause of a patient's miscarriages and Greengrass claims his car has been stolen. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy confronts Zoe about the Sugden fiasco. Bernice suggests to Mandy that she dresses more conservatively. Zoe tells Chris he may still have a chance with Kathy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laura tries to cheer Chris up at work. Stella moves into Home Farm and Eric offers to be her estate manager. Jack and Sarah are disgusted by Zoe's attempt at bribery

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider organises the protestors on the Red Rec. Ken tries hard to cheer Deirdre up. Alec goes to Switzerland to visit Vicky. Des aims to prove his commitment to Samantha

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley rows with Fred about Zoe. The Malletts return and Judy feels she will go mad knowing Katie is just across the street. Deirdre is formally charged by the police

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is delighted by the arrival of his older brother Sam, but their reunion turns to misery when Martin delivers bad news to one of them. Louisa searches for a new home in the village and visits Edith for a check-up, where she reveals an embarrassing piece of information, and Al makes his situation with Pauline worse by trying to act on advice from Bert. Starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al tries to raise some quick cash, and offers to sort out all Louisa's household and plumbing problems if she can pay him promptly. The teacher gets good news on the employment front, and Martin accidentally inflicts a minor wound on an elderly patient - who responds by walking out of the surgery and vowing never to return. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Phyllida Law and Benjamin Whitrow

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin fail to find a lead when a 13-year-old is kicked to death at Euston Station, so they turn to Chandler, who holds a press conference. As the detectives are led through a tangled web of family loyalties, the suspect's barrister, an old flame of Steel's, gathers evidence for an audacious defence strategy that could change the nature of the British legal system. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels

Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at what goes into orange squash and a profile of a family firm making brooms. Plus, an insight into the care that goes into making a Savile Row suit, and how trumpets are handcrafted. Ricky Tomlinson narrates

programme.desc: Richard Henwood presents action from obscure motorsports, such as bus and caravan racing and ice speedway

programme.desc: Arthur is held hostage by former friend Frankie Connor, who is now a prisoner on the run and desperate to know whether his wife is having an affair with Italian restaurateur Luigi. Final episode of the light-hearted drama, starring George Cole, Gary Webster and Glynn Edwards

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Hornblower's resourcefulness turns the attack on the Spanish-held fortress into a famous victory, but Buckland's treachery puts his life into further danger. Surviving a risky mission to blow up the fortifications and an attempt by the prisoners to take command of the Renown, the hero faces the death penalty for pushing Captain Sawyer into the hold - and hopes Archie's dying words can save him from the noose. Naval adventure, based on the stories by CS Forester, and starring Ioan Gruffudd

With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

Fate seems to smile on the ambitious seafarer though, when he discovers the villainous Bonaparte has reneged on the treaty. programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day seven at Celtic Manor resort in Newport, Wales, featuring the conclusion of the final. programme.title: Death Race (2008)

Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.desc: Howard decides to tell his mother that he and Bernadette are engaged - but she later collapses, making him fear the shock of the news has caused her to suffer a heart attack. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Selma realises her hopes of ever bearing a child are diminishing and flies to China to adopt a baby. programme.title: Sunday Brunch

Rochelle Humes talks about the Channel 4 documentary, The Black Maternity Scandal: Dispatches. Nick Helm talks about the screening of his show, Phoenix from the Flames. programme.title: Rango (2011)
programme.desc: A pet chameleon with an active imagination ends up lost in the desert. He makes his way to a lawless town of animals, where he is mistaken for a deadly gunslinger and made sheriff. He organises the townsfolk into a posse when bandits led by an evil rattlesnake steal their supply of water. Oscar-winning animated comedy, with the voices of Johnny Depp, Isla Fisher and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Ice Age (2002)
programme.desc: Three mismatched prehistoric animals join forces to return a human infant to its tribe - but as their journey unfolds, the creatures fail to anticipate the many perils and pitfalls that lay in store for them. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of John Leguizamo, Ray Romano, Denis Leary, Goran Visnjic and Jack Black

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge turn their attentions to the wash house - a shell of an old stone building on the far side of the moat. They have visions of turning it into a bathroom for their 40-foot boat on the moat, complete with all eco-friendly mod cons. And it's not long before the whole family get to work clearing the overgrown site so the project can begin

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: Featuring a ride on the stunning West Highland line, where an ingenious 19th-century invention is still protecting Scotland's railway from rockfall. Plus, a look at the profit to be made from collecting old railway memorabilia, the team tasked with cleaning Britain's largest station roof at Edinburgh Waverley, and follow artist Leo du Feu, who paints castles from trains

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the opening round of the campaign at Bahrain International Circuit. Lewis Hamilton of Mercedes went into this race as the man to beat, having prevailed here and gone on to win his seventh world title last year. Hamilton and rival Sebastian Vettel have dominated this race since 2012, with both drivers winning it on four seperate occasions. With analysis from David Coulthard and Mark Webber

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Following their win at the world championship, the now-separated Bellas (an all-female a cappella singing group) reunite for one last singing competition at an overseas USO tour. However, they face a squad who uses both instruments and voices. Final part of the blockbusting comedy trilogy, starring Anna Kendrick, Rebel Wilson and Brittany Snow

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Straight-talking Mel and partner Patrick host night one in the Lincolnshire town of Grimsby with a traditional menu, followed by seasoned ravers Amber and Ryan. Their goat's cheese starter is not to everyone's taste on the second evening, and tensions run high on the final night as hosts Vanessa and Steve won't take any rudeness from Mel. It's not long before sparks fly around the table

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: A frustrated Serena Joy becomes desperate, and the Commander tries to make amends. Nick pushes Eden further away, and Offred is faced with an unexpected reunion

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George's continued renovation mission takes him to Moseley and Wheaton Aston in the West Midlands. He begins with a conversion to unite upstairs and downstairs flats in an Arts and Crafts building, although one of his suggestions leaves the owners speechless. He also visits an 18th-century pub that has fallen victim to some dodgy DIY - and quickly devises a clever plan to make the most of the period features

programme.title: Fifteen to One
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the general knowledge quiz, in which 15 contestants are pitted against one another in the hope of securing a place in the end-of-series final when a £40,000 jackpot will be up for grabs

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Seven more couples board the coach, visiting some of southern Europe's leading party spots to win a luxury stay in Ibiza. Brendan Sheerin is back to act as tour guide and try to keep them in check, with the competition kicking off in Magaluf

programme.desc: George Clarke returns to Ireland to call on Chris and Rebecca, who swapped city life for self-sufficiency on a remote farm. Nine months on from his last visit, he finds they have just finished the final room in the house. The land is now yielding crops, Rebecca has begun selling chutneys, jams and vinegar, and Chris has plans to turn an outhouse into a farm shop

With Lisa not having been camping for 'about a hundred years', converting the quirky outbuilding into a viable B&B prospect, marking a voyage of discovery for her and Jules alike

programme.desc: A critically ill patient is brought into the emergency department after suffering a sudden cardiac arrest while in a local cafe with his wife. programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Craig Charles and Ortis Deley go camping to find out if you need to spend a fortune on high-end tents, sleeping bags and stoves to have a premium outdoors experience. Jon Bentley tests microwaves, and there is a feature on a lunchbox that can not only store your lunch, but heat it up as well

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: The naturalist walks 12 miles of Suffolk's shingle coast, starting at Minsmere Nature Reserve, and later heads through the protected wetlands at Snape Maltings in a kayak. Her final destination is the 10-mile-long shingle spit at Orford Ness, a former military test site which is now a National Trust nature reserve

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the life of one of Britain's most iconic Prime Ministers, Winston Churchill. It begins with his early years, when he is driven by the desire to prove himself to his father. He is born into aristocracy in the grand family home of Bleinhem Palace. From a young age he is acutely aware he has a lot to live up to. His father, Randolph Churchill, is an ambitious, controversial, highly successful politician and a strict disciplinarian who fails to give young Winston the affection he craves. Churchill never gets the chance to reconcile with his father and channels his sense of loss at his untimely death into a fierce determination to succeed at all costs

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Simon Bates narrates this look at the music of 1983, featuring interviews with Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini, Cheryl Baker, Carol Decker, Jenny Powell and other famous faces. Among the featured tracks are Eurythmics' Sweet Dreams (Are Made of This), New Order's Blue Monday, and Bad Boys by Wham!

programme.title: The Real Patrick Swayze: In His Own Words
programme.desc: Patrick Swayze had everything, from good looks and hit films to a stable and loving relationship. But he spent a lifetime battling the feeling that he wasn't good enough. More than a decade after his death, this is the inside story of his struggle for acceptance, by others and by himself

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi has been called to help Alan, tenanted landlord of the Crown Inn, nestled in the Wiltshire village of Aldbourne. Seven years ago, Alan took on the Crown hoping it would set him up for retirement. But now at 69 years old, he is still working every hour of every day just to get by. With the Crown failing to pull any profit, no pension in place and no Plan B, Alan is desperate for Alex's help

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross discovers who mugged him as a child and Chandler's internship gets off to an uninspiring start. Meanwhile, Joey endures a draining audition at the hands of a self-important actor. Guest starring Jeff Goldblum and Tenacious D's Kyle Gass

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Cash-strapped couple Chandler and Monica conceal from each other that they have asked Joey for money. programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Rookie team member Rachael faces a baptism of fire on a night shift, from a face-off with a driver threatening her colleague Lee's family, to the pursuit of a high-speed Mercedes carrying with a pregnant passenger. Elsewhere, Liam plays a pitch-black game of hunt the knife in the aftermath of a terrifying armed robbery, dog handler Mo deals with a double fire before arresting a man who appears to be stealing from a phone box, and a motorist picks up the phone and calls the police to report interceptor Chris for stealing his car

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A failure to stop in Hartlepool turns into an armed stand-off and a full-blown kidnapping situation, while a 14-year-old boy is found in possession of a bag of crack cocaine, cash and a knife. Lee goes on a high-speed pursuit through Stockton, and Jacko and Simon tackle a drug driver who is wearing a balaclava

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: AJ and Richie are in pursuit of a van speeding through narrow country lanes, while Jacko and the team box in a van carrying large quantities of cannabis. Sparks fly when dog handler Justin and police dog Elsa capture two burglars on a building site

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain, beginning with the invention of the steam locomotive and the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks. Chris then explores the role of the railways during the two world wars and looks at the devastating impact of the Beeching report in the 1960s

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Part two of two. The singer's trip to Australia and New Zealand concludes as she visits the North Island, known for its outdoor activities and adventures. She begins in Wellington, the capital of New Zealand, before travelling 900m above sea level to take part in quad-biking. The ship then sails north to Napier, where Jane takes a bus trip to meet a traditional Maori tribe, before arriving in Rotorua, a place famous for its dramatic landscapes. After a helicopter tour of Auckland, Jane's journey ends back in Sydney, where her final trek is to climb the 1,438 steps of the Sydney Harbour Bridge

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: The comedian takes a tour of the Cotswolds, beginning in Tetbury, where she gets some tips for a practice run for the market town's famous woolsack races, before moving on to Westonbirt, home to forest bathing and a club devoted to puddings. Susan moves on to Bourton-on-the-Water miniature village, makes a spoon under the guidance of a special silversmith, and explores the gardens of an Indian palace in the midst of the Cotswolds

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Singapore establishment once known as the Savoy of the East, whose guests range from royalty to rock stars, and from politicians to poets - plus the occasional tiger. Recently re-opened after a multi-million-dollar refurbishment, the hotel boasts stories from the past that are as exotic as the location itself, with an army of staff who treat their 5-star guests to 5-star service. Here, head butler Grace tells her tales of the boudoir and head doorman Narajan explains how first impressions count

programme.title: Grace Kelly's Missing Millions
programme.desc: When former actress and princess of Monaco died, her assets seemingly amounted to just $10,000 and a ramshackle cottage in Ireland. in this programme, financial guru Gemma Godfrey sifts through documents and crunches the numbers to find out the millions made and lost by the actress, revealing where Princess Grace's fortune ultimately went

programme.title: Princess Margaret: Rebel Without a Crown
programme.desc: A profile of the Queen's younger sister, who was determined to live her life exactly as she wanted, even if that sometimes meant bringing the monarchy into disrepute. As a child she was as a mischievous and rebellious princess who loved being the centre of attention, while when she split up with photographer Anthony Armstrong-Jones she became the first major royal to get divorced so since Henry VIII

programme.title: The Story of
programme.desc: The life and times of the artist who has enjoyed more number one hits than any other British solo artist in chart history. Heavily influenced by his hero Elvis Presley in his younger years, Cliff also starred in a string of fun-loving cinema classics such as The Young Ones and Summer Holiday. He has always remained true to his deeply held Christian beliefs, and once memorably entertained the crowds at Wimbledon when rain stopped play. Here, friends, colleagues and journalists discuss his fascinating life, illustrated with performance and interview archive from every period of his career
