programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The production of inflatable toys, couscous and modelling dough, and how these methods have evolved over the years

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Mature student Dave takes his turn to pose for the group, and Stew is keen to try to produce a technically perfect painting. Glenn gets experimental with his colours and Amanda goes all out with a variety of mediums in a bid to win Dave's vote. Presented by Jenny Eclair, with art mentor Diana Ali

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A pair of British sailing instructors start a new life on the Spanish coast, and a new ale could prove to be an epic fail for brewers in France

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Angela and Steve from Leicestershire search for a second home in Lanzarote that they can move to permanently after retirement, with a budget of £225,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi shows Essex-born Jackie and her Portuguese partner Hilario a selection of properties that fall within their £62,000 budget in the Eastern Algarve, where the couple want to buy their dream holiday home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Richard Fall and Laura Howes are thinking of settling down to start a family, but are torn between buying a large home in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, near where they live, or purchasing an apartment in Valencia on Spain's eastern coast

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks compete for the £1,000 prize in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and keen basketball player Ken Oputa is the first to host, inviting his guests to turn up in sportswear fancy dress. He hopes his Double Dribble mushrooms and Slam Dunk salmon will prove to be winning ingredients

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer Erika Sager hosts the second dinner party of the week on board her boat in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and invites her guests to dress up as pirates. Despite the unconventional setting, her rivals are all impressed by the standard of her food and hospitality

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-two Sarah Brice hosts the third dinner party in Kingston upon Thames, hoping to impress her guests with her own take on dishes including prawns, lamb and chocolate mousse. Ken Oputa tries to make an impression on the female contestants with his romantic poetry, but finds Erika Sager switches her attentions to his rival Jamie Baker

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Jamie Baker hosts the fourth party in London, and his ambitious menu of langoustine and crab ravioli, herb crusted rack of lamb, and milloux of chocolate - combined with his choice of evening entertainment - go down well, until an unexpected turn of events scuppers his plans

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired golfer David Witcher hosts the final evening in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and hopes to impress with an American-themed menu, including Wyoming slow-cooked ribs and Louisiana seafood chowder. After dinner, he entertains his guests with a bucking bronco, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to transform the fortunes of Sebastian's, a pizzeria in Burbank, Los Angeles, which is failing to attract customers, leaving the owner in debt and struggling to support his family. Gordon is horrified to discover pre-processed and frozen ingredients on the menu, but there seems to be little he can do to help when he is thrown out of the kitchen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Joanne and Dean from Hertfordshire have been together for six years and have been enjoying holidays in Tenerife ever since. They love it so much that they've decided to take the next step and buy their very own holiday home in Costa Adeje, so call upon Ben Hillman to find five properties for them to choose between, within their budget of £150,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps hairdresser Kim and car salesman Steve find their first property together. Kim and Steve have been together for six years and have holidayed several times on the Spanish island of Gran Canaria. With a budget of £120,000, they can't wait to turn their dream of a romantic bolthole into a reality

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect and presenter looks back over a selection of projects that celebrate Britain's rich history and traditions, including a pair of bunkers from the Second World War in a Surrey garden and a Victorian railway carriage containing rare mahogany from the 1600s. Plus, a doghouse in Devon being converted into an extension and the ex-cop launching a cream tea business in a camper

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In this art deco special, George takes on a challenging period property in Newquay, owned by Gareth and Frances Jenkins. The building was once an architectural gem, but over the years has been the victim of unsympathetic modernisations, making it unrecognisable from its 1930s heyday. The challenge is to strip away the ugly accretions to reveal the house in all its glory - while also making it suitable for modern family living. Plus, George visits a stunning seaside home and Britain's biggest collection of neon signs

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: A 23-year-old man with a history of violence is questioned over a string of offences that have left victims badly hurt, but refuses to give any answers. Without fresh evidence, the police has no choice but to release the suspect. Within days of leaving police custody, he reoffends and officially ends up on the Most Wanted list

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, 30-year-old Pablo, who is HIV positive and hopes to break the stigma about his condition, and Leon, a musician and rapper from Burnley, take part

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: MC Dizzee Rascal, radio DJ Nick Grimshaw, psychologist Philippa Perry and actor-writer Reece Shearsmith brave the baking tent in the name of charity. All that stands between them and the title of Star Baker are three challenges - vegetable slices, a 'right royal' technical and a biscuit selfie depicting themselves at work. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith judge their efforts, while Matt Lucas shouts out how much time is remaining in a silly voice

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: There's discord between contestants Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall as rows erupt over facial hair and Jamali's driving skills. Taskmaster Greg Davies is likely pleased that his usual divide-and-conquer tactics are seemingly paying early dividends, as he watches Mike vaulting a high fence, Sarah wearing shoes on her knees, Charlotte grappling with static electricity and Lee struggling to see a balloon and a ghost. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary set inside Wood Green animal charity, following efforts to match dogs with new owners. Nine-year-old Rohan meets Patterdale terrier puppy Friday, but there are concerns that the hound may be just a bit too enthusiastic for the youngster. Rocco the Jack Russell has been struggling to find a home, though his bouncy exuberance and love of a good face lick means he could be the perfect match for Zach, who is looking for a dog not fazed by his Tourette's. Grant the stray westie has seen some tough times, but his luck might be about to change with the arrival of Casper and Sid

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Bowlees Farm Holiday Cottages in Co Durham, where Tony and Viv Smith hope that their tranquil setting, hot tubs and indoor swimming pool impresses

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Piebald Inn in Hunmanby, North Yorkshire, where the lack of a thermostat and a divisive design idea irritate some of the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Stoneleigh House Hotel, a boutique townhouse in the heart of Buckingham run by Jane Parslow and Rachel Rawding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final venue is glamping site Evenlode Grounds in the Cotswolds, owned by Howard and Amanda Wooliams, who impress their guests by revealing it is also a working farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up to fire back on their feedback are Tony and Viv, followed by John and Lesley, who are both desperate to know why Jane and Rachel were the only two who wouldn't return to their B&Bs

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shares a simple recipe that takes the humble aubergine and gives it a delicious Italian twist. He transforms it into an incredible aubergine Milanese with a sweet tomato, basil and garlic sauce, topped with grated Parmesan. The chef also rustles up his tasty, crispy salmon tacos, with Cajun-rubbed salmon and a mango salsa, wrapped in a toasted tortilla for a quick and colourful lunch

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two restaurateurs from Wales try to turn a rundown townhouse into a boutique B&B in Spain. And there's drama for a West Sussex couple juggling a guesthouse and a new bar in the Spanish countryside

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two B&B hosts in Spain discover their property is a money-pit, there is trouble for France's novice beer-makers, and a guesthouse and bar leaves a West Sussex couple stressed

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A pair of British sailing instructors start a new life on the Spanish coast, and a new ale could prove to be an epic fail for brewers in France

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: B&B hosts from Wales prepare for VIP guests, new brewers in France try to keep up with increasing demand for their British beer, and two sailing instructors try to still afloat among a tide of competition on the Spanish coast

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the summer comes to an end, two British sailors get shipshape for a big inspection. The dream comes to fruition for B&B hosts in Spain, and two new brewers in France risk being a victim of their own success as the beer barrels run dry

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is on the Canary Island of Tenerife helping husband and wife Teresa and Robin find their dream holiday home that they hope to retire to in the future. Having visited the island six times in the last few years, it's quickly become their favourite place. With five options lined up for their £120,000 budget, will they find what they're looking for?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: With autumn just around the corner, it's a chance for the Strawbridge family to make the most of the wild larder on their doorstep. Dick is determined to make sloe gin, while Angel presses on with her plan to create a library in the pepper pot tower

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two shows that highlighted the need to get the balance between home and garden just right. In Bury St Edmunds, Lauren Grant believes the opportunity to build on a land she and husband Christian bought from the council will transform their confused downstairs. He, however, just wants rid of the house altogether. Meanwhile, Harrogate couple Carol and Andrew need extra space for Grandad. Can Kirstie build into their sizeable garden or will Phil find somewhere else more suitable?

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred and his team of matchmakers invite more singletons to the luxury hotel in the hope of finding love in the sun. A string of unhappy relationships has left 24-year-old Thalia with a phobia of kissing. Her date with 25-year-old Essex lad Jack date gets off to a great start after a discovery of a shared love of sharks. Widower Tony is looking for someone to share his twilight years and is paired with the glamorous Lesley, a fellow East Ender. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hoteliers head to Southport, where husband and wife Rick and Sarah Blaney run the Stamford. However, things do not get off to a great start when Annelies and Claudia are left out in the cold by the self check-in

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother-and-daughter team Annelies and Claudia Richardson host the week's last visit at the Fauconberg Arms in Coxwold, North Yorkshire. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On Payment Day, Carl is keen to quiz Simon and Joe over their breakfast woes, while Rick and Sarah want to know why they were scored so badly for their hosting skills. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. Members of staff complain their pay cheques bounce, and the kitchen cooler is so unhygienic that Gordon makes the decision to close the restaurant on Valentine's Day - the busiest night of the year

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to help a struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, where the three owners regularly come into conflict as they each try to assert their authority over the business, causing confusion for all. When one of the trio stubbornly resists the changes the Scottish chef is hoping to implement, sparks begin to fly

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, J Willy's Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, which has amassed debts of more than $1million. On finding an inexperienced chef serving food from both the freezer and tins, together with service that reduces one customer to tears, Gordon Ramsay orders an overnight deep-clean of the premises and designs a menu to inspire the kitchen staff

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Software engineer Andy Forsyth and his partner Jacqui Monroe from Yorkshire have three children between them. With the youngest about to fly the nest, they've decided the time is right to follow their dream and buy a second home in Barbados. Danni Menzies shows them five fantastic properties for their £400,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Steve and Dawn first visited Mijas on the eastern coast of Spain 11 years ago and have wanted to own a holiday home there ever since. They eventually plan to live in Spain, so the pressure is on for Laura Hamilton to show them five alternate properties within their maximum budget of £220,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Music therapist Sarah Hadley from Surrey has a long-term love affair with the Greek island of Crete, a place she and her best friend Sue Williams have been visiting together for years. Now, Sarah is after her perfect Cretan holiday home and has asked Sue to help her with the decision. Jasmine Harman shows them five incredible properties for a budget of £150,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Denise and Roger have fallen in love with the Breton culture and lifestyle. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties in north-west France for their £120,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Susan Peters from Dorchester, Dorset, has her heart set on moving to stunning Mallorca. Armed with a £130,000 budget and with best friend Tina to help her with the decision, Scarlette Douglas shows her five properties on the island

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: The second episode explores the coronation of George VI in the aftermath of the succession crisis, and the efforts of the uncertain new monarch to rebuild the prestige of a troubled monarchy as the nation faced the prospect of being drawn into the Second World War

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: A 23-year-old man with a history of violence is questioned over a string of offences that have left victims badly hurt, but refuses to give any answers. Without fresh evidence, the police has no choice but to release the suspect. Within days of leaving police custody, he reoffends and officially ends up on the Most Wanted list

programme.title: Strangers Making Babies
programme.desc: There are currently 70,000 people signed up to co-parenting sites, looking for platonic partners to have children with - without the added complication of finding love first. This series follows a group of single women as they are matched with several men, guided by fertility specialist Marie Wren and matchmaking expert Gillian McCallum as they spend time together and work out whether or not to have a baby together. Up first are professional nanny Venicia, who longs to have a child of her own, and amateur ballroom dancer Sarah

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A small cavachon is rushed to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick after being hit by a car. She has a broken leg, but also a serious heart condition. Meanwhile, Simba, a one-year-old Labrador needs help with her badly formed hips, and Tulee the nervous basset hound has diseased discs in her back that leave her in constant pain

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick offers cutting-edge treatment for Luca, a long-suffering four-year-old springer spaniel with a painful elbow disease. He designs and creates a special screw for Luca's leg with a system for injecting a bone-growth gel around it, and after an eight-year-old Newfoundland collapses, its owners face an agonising wait for the MRI scans to reveal if anything can be done for the gentle giant

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: A five-tonne bull elephant is moved to a new home. On the Western Cape, marine biologist Alison prepares to release three critically endangered African penguins back into the ocean

programme.title: Rich Kids, Skint Holiday
programme.desc: Well-to-do teens are taught the true value of money by swapping their first-class globetrotting getaways for micro-budget breaks in the UK. Mel is accustomed to at least three 5-star holidays a year, all paid for by her family. Now she'll be ditching her Louboutins for a pair of wellies on a camping holiday with experienced budgeteers Pam and Graham, their 23-year-old daughter Anne-Marie and three-year-old grandson Jayden from Walsall

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: A decade of health problems has resulted in Elaine's two-bedroom flat in Rochester, Kent, being in dire need of a deep clean, but cat sick on the floor and an infestation of fleas make it one of the toughest jobs that Jon and Brennan have ever taken on

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A doctor is forced to call in an eviction lawyer when his tenant, who owes him thousands of pounds in arrears, refuses to leave. Brent council officers conduct a raid in north-west London, finding more than 30 men living in a single house, and a landlady inspects her property after her tenant, an extreme hoarder, dies

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Mansfield mother-of-two Kerrie was left feeling betrayed after renting her house to a friend in need, who then stopped paying any rent. To add insult to injury, she also ruined the house by keeping too many dogs at the property. Mental health nurse Emmanuel found himself in a financial hole when his tenant stopped paying rent or answering his calls, and a landlord in Notting Hill ended up being owed £16,000 after five months of receiving no rent

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly £2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost £3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team are outnumbered and outmuscled when they stop a coach-load of cage fighters following an anonymous tip off that there may be drugs on board. A suspected wheel thief kicks off on the carriageway. When Interceptors Kev and Andy pull over a Peugeot, the occupants are in no mood to hang around. Finally, a drunk dices with death as he walks the wrong way up the middle of the busy A19

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev and Steve go on the hunt for suspected heroin dealers, while dog handler Liam attends the scene of a suspected arson attack. Damo deals with a drunk driver, and another man is so inebriated behind the steering wheel that he blows three-and-a-half times over the limit

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Justin Moffitt and Paul Faulkner chase down a gang of suspected car thieves, while it takes seven officers to stop a violent thief who tries to sink his teeth into them. Elsewhere, a drug raid turns life-threatening, and dog-handler Jimmy Grieves tries to find out what has happened to a man with a bloodied face

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben try to drill through the locks on a property to evict the residents, only to find to their surprise that the door has been modified with special reinforced metal. Delroy and Brian try to reclaim money a garage owes to a customer and are shocked by the condition of the vehicle in question, and a woman who has acted as guarantor for her sister's tenancy does not realise the extent of her responsibilities

programme.desc: John and Susie strategise as to how they might win the election. Lewis wonders if his suspicions and thoughts about Christian have caused more harm than good

programme.title: Cats and Dogs at War
programme.desc: Animal experts Chirag Patel and Nanci Creedon help families whose fighting pets have turned their homes into a battlefield

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Letting agent Paul uncovers a cannabis farm during a routine arrears inspection, but learns that the tenant has been in prison for the past four months and claims ignorance of the criminal crop. Meanwhile, builders Stuart and Steve win a possession order to regain their property, but face the worst damage they have ever seen and a £20,000 bill to repair it

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The story of Lesley and Mark Irwin, who fell foul of a single mother and confidence trickster. After several months, the tenant fell behind with the rent and it was discovered she had advertised the home as a rental property and stole deposits from unwitting students hoping to move into the area. Plus, a family living in a cramped and cold multiple-occupancy house in south London, and have to share a bathroom and kitchen with other tenants, seek help from Lewisham Council

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord offers a former tenant the use of a spare room until she gets back on her feet, but then struggles to evict her - and things get even more difficult when the woman moves her partner in. A landlord travelling abroad has to return to London when her tenants stop paying the rent, and Harrow Council officers find 30 mattresses in a flat designed for four people

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Mansfield mother-of-two Kerrie was left feeling betrayed after renting her house to a friend in need, who then stopped paying any rent. To add insult to injury, she also ruined the house by keeping too many dogs at the property. Mental health nurse Emmanuel found himself in a financial hole when his tenant stopped paying rent or answering his calls, and a landlord in Notting Hill ended up being owed £16,000 after five months of receiving no rent

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly £2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost £3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: One-off edition in which the High Court enforcement agents are caught in the crossfire of three bitter family disputes, including a debt-dodging man who pushes his panic-stricken mother over the edge. Plus, a family booted onto the streets at night by a ruthless aunt, and a man who won't engage with the agents and loses his car

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In north London, agents Del and Dael chase nearly £2,000 owed by a printing company, but the owner denies the debt and starts getting physical. Meanwhile, Stewart and Elmor find themselves caught up in a personal dispute when they try to collect rent arrears from a barber's shop, and in Bedfordshire, Del and Brian try to recover almost £2,000 owed to a funeral director. Stewart and Vic track down a debt of £5,000 from the owner of a computer repair shop in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Experienced enforcement agent Stewart and trainee Iain head to Blackpool to chase down a builder owing nearly £3,500 to an unhappy customer, while father and son Del and Dael are in Essex to collect over £2,500 owed to a frozen food company. In south London, Del and Brian attempt to recover nearly £10,000 from a debtor who acted as guarantor for a friend's rental agreement, ending in a bizarre stand-off with the debtor's daughter

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Another selection of residential feuds, featuring Barnsley pensioners Barry and Phyllis Roddis's dispute with a neighbour who was scrapping cars in his driveway, which led to him waging a nine-year campaign of intimidation against them. Plus, in Streatham, south London, Richard and Philomena Chadwick have been battling for five years to evict squatters whose bongo-playing parties and rooftop sunbathing have invaded the privacy of their once secluded garden

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The De Ropers' hopes of a peaceful life in Yeovil, Somerset, were rudely shattered by their neighbour Peter Stoodley's 120 noisy chickens, and they spent their first two years in their new home battling to persuade the authorities to ban him from keeping animals. In a quiet Anglesey village, 67-year-old Pamela Booth's discovered that her neighbour had been fraudulently using her bank card to steal more than £1,000 over 10 months, while a 24-year-old wannabe rapper in Dagenham, east London, generated more than 80 complaints after repeatedly keeping residents awake by playing loud music into the early hours in his flat

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: In the Worcestershire village of Broadway, two families were taken advantage of by a cafe owner to whom they lent £120,000 between them, each unaware that they were not the sole investor. In Bristol, Tim Wrigley ended up being on the receiving end of intimidation and abuse after tiring of his neighbour's increasing demands and bizarre gifts, while a Nottingham mother and son resort to filming their noisy neighbour after months of endless loud music, shouting and personal threats

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: American serial killer Bill Suff, who is thought to have murdered between 12 and 22 people during an 18-year spree. Described as a mild-mannered loner, Suff raped, stabbed, strangled, and sometimes mutilated prostitutes in California, beginning in 1986. In January 1992, he was arrested after a routine traffic stop

programme.title: Murdered By
programme.desc: Crime documentary about victims killed by people known to them, beginning with north London transgender woman Naomi Hersi, murdered in March 2018 by a man she found on a dating website

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: Trainee accountant Stuart Harling was obsessed with an ultraviolent video game, and in 2006 killed nurse Cheryl Moss by stabbing her 72 times. A-level student Steven Miles watched nothing but crime drama Dexter, and in 2014 dismembered his girlfriend. Aaron Roach Bridgeman talks with experts associated with the cases, contemplating whether any connection can be proven between entertainment and actions

programme.title: The Disappearance
programme.desc: With the mystery behind the disappearance finally becoming clear, the police and the Sullivan family face a race against time. Drama, starring Peter Coyote

programme.desc: Critical Care Paramedic Fay is despatched to the scene of a serious motorbike crash, and Ambulance control receive an emergency call reporting a man collapsed in a churchyard

Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the murder of a doctor at a fertility clinic, but are embroiled in a dispute between two women over the ownership of frozen embryos. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The execution-style murder of a prison guard triggers an examination of the corruption apparently inherent in the state's penal system. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Angie Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An investigation into three murders in the offices of a magazine places the detectives in the middle of a fierce family feud, involving a protective matriarch, warring siblings and a corrupt judge. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Police officer Danny Pelham is murdered while staking out the apartment of a wanted man's girlfriend. The felon he was trying to snare becomes the chief suspect, and Briscoe and Curtis eventually track him down, only to discover the murdered cop had a history of violence against his girlfriend. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates when a respected journalist is shot shortly after writing a hard-hitting article on the New York drugs scene. It soon emerges that the story was fabricated and had earned the reporter his fair share of enemies, while an unexpected lead causes the officers to reopen a 20-year-old case concerning the murder of a famous actress. Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A hidden anti-Semitic message in a high-school yearbook offers an insight into the murder of an art teacher, and the subsequent case leads McCoy into conflict with 'Klan lawyer' Roy Payne

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ziva confronts her past when the team is ordered to protect her father, Mossad director Eli David, at a gathering of former heads of NCIS. The agents discover he is being targeted by terrorists who have hijacked a shipment of advanced weapons from the army. Drama, guest starring TJ Ramini and Michael Nouri, with Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When the lead suspect of an unsolved and high-profile robbery case is released from prison, the NCIS team members resume their investigation with an undercover stakeout

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: After a reporter and his fiancee are gunned down outside a restaurant, Goren and Eames are given a seemingly reliable eyewitness description of the killer. However, while on the trail of their suspect, they discover the journalist was planning to publish an expose on corruption within the sanitation industry, taking their investigation in an entirely new direction

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon reports the disappearance of his wife, and Goren suspects there is more to the case than meets the eye, but struggles to find any flaws in the doctor's alibi. When body parts are found washed up on a beach, the detectives set a trap, placing a story in the papers claiming this could be the missing woman, to see how her husband reacts. Donna Hanover guest stars

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mac continues the investigation into the killing of Carver's sister and discovers a bullet lodged in the corpse's chest that matches a gun registered to the chief. However, Jo warns him of the consequences of accusing a senior officer of murder. Starring Gary Sinise and John Larroquette

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Jo discover the body of a department store manager in a shop window when they apprehend a pickpocket. They suspect the thief of murder, but his excuse leads them to the victim's assistant, who appears to have planned the crime. However, they unearth evidence that her dead boss had been accused of sexual harassment and had wounds on his body indicating that he might also have been an assailant. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.desc: Jessica learns that her friend Seth Hazlitt's rich cousin has been murdered and vows to get to the bottom of the mystery - and when she discovers the victim was breeding rare magnolias, she soon finds a motive for the crime. Angela Lansbury and William Windom star

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tries to clear a friend implicated in the theft of a priceless Egyptian relic - and a murder to boot. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury, James Read and Lee Meriwether

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits Genoa in Italy, where she finds her sleuthing skills called upon in the world of opera when a soprano is threatened by a stranger hidden in the foggy night. Murder mystery, starring Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A scientist hits on the cunning idea of faking his own death, but is later murdered for real - leaving Jessica to prove the innocence of the police's chief suspect. Shannon Tweed guest stars, with Angela Lansbury, Bradford Dillman and Bruce Weitz

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica discovers a link between the death of a film fan and the supposed suicide of a leading Hollywood director 35 years previously. Murder mystery, starring Angela Lansbury and John Astin

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica enters the world of haute couture to solve a series of murders committed in the seedy surroundings of a Parisian sweatshop, and the lover of a model who helped her research a book is targeted by a killer in Paris. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The beleaguered Cabot Cove residents fall victim to a callous development scheme - all except Jessica, who resolves to get to the bottom of the mystery. Angela Lansbury stars as the amateur sleuth, with William Windom, Ron Masak and Michael Horton

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The heads of an Ivy League private college tempt fate by inviting Jessica to lecture to their pupils, and it is not long before the sleuth has a murder to investigate. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: While Jessica is enjoying her stay at a fishing lodge in Ireland, she finds out the owner is being harassed by a consortium wanting to buy his land for a strip mine. Whodunit, guest starring Rod Taylor and Martin Jarvis, with Angela Lansbury

programme.desc: When the SVU investigates a sexual assault case involving two students in a special needs school, Rollins discovers that one child's medical issues stem from a surprising source. Detective drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Kelli Giddish

programme.desc: A pilot hijacks a commercial plane to escape from the man who attacked her, and Barba targets the airline for driving her to drastic action with an abusive working culture. Meanwhile, Fin takes steps to ensure Benson is protected at work

programme.title: Columbo: Negative Reaction (1974)
programme.desc: A successful photographer wants to get rid of his nagging wife so he can pursue an affair with his assistant. He murders his spouse and makes it look as if she was killed by a kidnapper, and at the same time puts an ex-con in the frame. However, the dogged detective sees through his ruse and proceeds to pick the story to pieces. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Dick Van Dyke, Don Gordon and Antoinette Bower

programme.title: Columbo: Now You See Him (1976)
programme.desc: A stage magician has kept the fact that he is a Nazi war criminal a secret for years. When a nightclub manager threatens to expose him, the illusionist kills him and arranges the perfect alibi - because at the time of the murder, he was apparently performing an escapology act. However, Columbo sets out to uncover the truth. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Jack Cassidy

programme.title: Columbo Goes to College (1991)
programme.desc: Two criminology students murder their professor and cover their tracks with false clues. They think they are on the way to outwitting everyone - but find themselves at the bottom of the class when Columbo intervenes. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Stephen Caffrey, Gary Hershberger and Robert Culp

programme.title: Columbo: Murder - A Self Portrait (1989)
programme.desc: A possessive painter who fears his ex-wife will expose his dark secrets resorts to killing her, but makes it appear she drowned. However, Columbo is unconvinced and draws on his broad palette of experience to reveal the truth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Patrick Bauchau and Fionnula Flanagan

programme.title: Columbo: Any Old Port in a Storm (1973)
programme.desc: A vineyard owner's financial security is jeopardised when his playboy younger brother threatens to sell the family winery. He resorts to killing his sibling and rigging the death scene to look like a scuba-diving accident. However, the crumpled cop sees through the ruse and in the process reveals he is something of a wine buff himself. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Donald Pleasence and Julie Harris

programme.title: Columbo: Ashes to Ashes (1998)
programme.desc: The shabby sleuth investigates the complex case of a murdered Hollywood gossip writer. He uses his unique talents to interrogate a smooth-talking funeral director, who was once romantically involved with the victim and one of the last people to see her alive. Detective drama, with Peter Falk. Patrick McGoohan co-stars and directs

programme.title: Columbo Likes the Nightlife (2003)
programme.desc: The disheveled cop is drawn into the world of the Los Angeles club scene when he investigates the apparent suicide of a tabloid reporter, and uncovers evidence of a blackmail plot. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Matthew Rhys and Jennifer Sky

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A right-wing public speaker is raped after one of her public appearances is disrupted by a protest

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls shoot a music video on a tropical island, but their presence angers a volcano god, who demands a human sacrifice

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls try to find a movie star's lost Chihuahuas while a storm is brewing

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Kong, Jason, jungle girl Lua, fun-loving Tann and wise Dr. Jenkins take on power-hungry scientists, ferocious prehistoric creatures and incredible magical beings to protect their unique island home.

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby challenges Vic to a climbing contest, with the winner joining the crew of the Drakkar

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Faxe steals sausages from an Icelandic king, which results in the whole crew getting held to ransom

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era

programme.title: TransMilitary (2018)
programme.desc: At a time when transgender people are banned from serving in the US military, four transgender troops risking discharge fight to attain the freedom they so fiercely protect

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Dentist on the Job (1961)
programme.desc: Two newly qualified dentists unwittingly get involved in the invention and promotion of a new toothpaste. Comedy, starring Bob Monkhouse, Ronnie Stevens, Kenneth Connor, Shirley Eaton, Eric Barker, Richard Wattis and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Kind Hearts and Coronets (1949)
programme.desc: The penniless grandson of a duke plots to eliminate the eight relatives standing between him and a vast inheritance. He shoots, drowns, poisons and explodes his way through his family tree, but his final triumph is ruined when his lady love's husband kills himself. Ealing black comedy, starring Alec Guinness as all eight potential victims, with Dennis Price, Valerie Hobson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Miracle in Soho (1957)
programme.desc: A cynical road-repairer falls for the alluring charms of an Italian girl working as a barmaid, only to discover she and her family are planning to move to Canada. Romantic drama written by Emeric Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Belinda Lee and Cyril Cusack

programme.title: Paper!
programme.desc: A piece of London history. Shot in the 1960's on 8mm film featuring the many news vendors around London selling the three evening newspapers

programme.title: The Knowledge: The World's Toughest Taxi Test
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the test that all black cab drivers working in the city of London must take, one of the world's toughest exams - The Knowledge of London. Each candidate must memorise the capital's 25,000 streets, traffic restrictions and ever-changing points of interest, which takes an average of four years of determination to successfully pass

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: Angelica (2017)
programme.desc: A couple in Victorian London endure an unusual series of supernatural happenings following the birth of their child. Drama, starring Jena Malone, Janet McTeer and Ed Stoppard

programme.title: Perfect Skin (2018)
programme.desc: Katia Matuschak, an 18-year-old Polish woman, embarks on a relationship with mysterious tattoo artist Bob Reid. However, their relationship takes a dark turn as her fascination with him grows, and in an attempt to fulfil his dream, he kidnaps her. Psychological horror, starring Richard Brake, Natalia Kostrzewa and Jo Woodcock

programme.title: Desperate Characters (1971)
programme.desc: A burglary turns a middle-aged couple's life together upside down, and makes them question the fundamental values they've always believed in. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Kenneth Mars, Gerald O'Loughlin and Sada Thompson

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: An insight into the crimes of the Peaky Blinders, the Birmingham-based gang that eventually forged ties with the Krays as part of the flourishing gang culture of the 1960s

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the coast at Boscastle and visits Ramsey on the Isle of Man

programme.title: The Man From Nowhere (1975)
programme.desc: A Victorian orphan is sent to live with her uncle, and is troubled by a ghostly presence that haunts his house. Supernatural mystery, starring Sarah Hollis Andrews and Ronald Adam

programme.title: Account Rendered (1957)
programme.desc: Police find no end of suspects with strong motives when they investigate the murder of a wealthy woman. Mystery, starring Griffith Jones and Honor Blackman

programme.title: Take My Life (1947)
programme.desc: A devoted wife journeys to Scotland in a bid to prove her husband has been wrongly accused of murdering his former girlfriend. Thriller, with Greta Gynt, Hugh Williams, Marius Goring and Francis L Sullivan

programme.title: Manganinnie (1980)
programme.desc: An Australian Aboriginal woman goes in search of her lost tribe, accompanied on her journey by a lost white girl. with whom she forms a close bond despite having no common language. As their journey towards the coast continues, the woman begins to suspect her people and culture may have been all but wiped out by British colonists. Historical drama, starring Mawuyul Yanthalawuy and Anna Ralph

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis

programme.title: Henry V (1989)
programme.desc: The English king leads his armies into war to reclaim control of France - culminating in a pivotal confrontation with much larger enemy forces on the fields of Agincourt. Adaptation of Shakespeare's historical play, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Derek Jacobi, Brian Blessed, Emma Thompson, Ian Holm, Robert Stephens and a young Christian Bale

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.

programme.title: Churchill's Traitors
programme.desc: An in-depth look at those who betrayed Winston Churchill during the Second World War, and the events that led to the attack on Pearl Harbour in December 1941

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: An American woman and a comedian's girlfriend both do their utmost to get into Oxford university to study the works of Lord Byron - but what will they do to gain a place at the great seat of learning? Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Dawn French, Lenny Henry and Nigel Planer

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael tries to get Joe Pesci to star in his proposed new film Bongo in the Congo, but receives an unenthusiastic response. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Occupation
programme.desc: The incredible true story of Sara Góralnik who at age 13 survived the Holocaust by taking refuge in a small Ukrainian village and passing as a Christian after her entire family was killed by Nazis

programme.title: Spivs (2004)
programme.desc: A professional con-man spends his time honing the skills of his protege and making sure his gang of conniving criminals stays out of trouble. However, when a simple heist backfires the ragtag group are left with a lorry-load of illegal immigrants on their hands - and an angry smuggler with revenge on his mind soon adds to their woes. Crime drama, starring Ken Stott,, Nick Moran and Dominic Monaghan

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador whose cancer is eating away its front leg, by replacing its tumour with a custom-made implant, and uses the same technique to save the knee of a border collie. Senior surgeon Michael Hamilton also tends a chinchilla that needs urgent treatment on its arm bones after falling from a shelf

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring and revealing New Cross, Eltham, and Catford

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s. This edition focuses on the story of glam rock. Featuring David Bowie, Alice Cooper, Jethro Tull and Elton John

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: The landmark series from the 1970s concludes with a look at the role of spirituality and religion in rock

programme.title: Skid Kids (1953)
programme.desc: A group of kids takes on a gang of bike thieves, with a little help from the police. Children's drama, starring Barry MacGregor and Anthony Lang

programme.title: Hell Is a City (1960)
programme.desc: A tough criminal escapes from jail and hatches a scheme to rob a wealthy bookie, little realising a hardened copper is hot on his trail. Thriller, starring Stanley Baker, John Crawford, Donald Pleasence and Billie Whitelaw

programme.title: Piccadilly Incident (1946)
programme.desc: A Wren is pronounced missing in action, but returns three years later to find her husband has remarried. Melodrama, starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Kind Hearts and Coronets (1949)
programme.desc: The penniless grandson of a duke plots to eliminate the eight relatives standing between him and a vast inheritance. He shoots, drowns, poisons and explodes his way through his family tree, but his final triumph is ruined when his lady love's husband kills himself. Ealing black comedy, starring Alec Guinness as all eight potential victims, with Dennis Price, Valerie Hobson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: The Big Shilling
programme.desc: A slice of London social history as crane & ball expert George Lovegrove recalls his life balling buildings and his family history of a dynasty of demolition men

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Next Big Thing
programme.desc: International Arts Judge James Nicholls is searching for emerging painters, sculptors, photographers and street artists to help them develop

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Mike steadfastly resists a management attempt to make the staff wear identical uniforms, convinced it's a move designed to rob them of their individuality _ but a visit from Anne's surprisingly attractive mother soon diverts his attention to more personal matters. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Susan Earl and Ken Morley

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: Desperate Characters (1971)
programme.desc: A burglary turns a middle-aged couple's life together upside down, and makes them question the fundamental values they've always believed in. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Kenneth Mars, Gerald O'Loughlin and Sada Thompson

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: An insight into how the cessation of open hostilities between Unionist and Nationalists factions in Northern Ireland led to an underground war between militant groups

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Focusing on renowned villains Eddie and Billy Blundell, this episode also delves into the murky world of rave culture

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard O'Mahoney explores the injustices suffered by those who have unintentionally surrounded themselves with criminals

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael tries to get Joe Pesci to star in his proposed new film Bongo in the Congo, but receives an unenthusiastic response. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 47-year-old man receives a shock when consultant surgeon Shaw Somers reveals he is suffering from metabolic syndrome and his body is more like that of a 60-year-old. A 23-year-old woman seeks help with her extremely bad breath, GP Giovanni Miletto puts together an experimental plan to combat chronic fatigue syndrome, and Pixie McKenna discovers how diet can influence people's chances of developing cancer

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Brixton and Harmondsworth for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks up The Great Orme in North Wales and beside a railway in West Yorkshire

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

Coverage of the opening match of the 2021 season from Emerald Headingley, as last year's Grand Final winners kick-off their title defence. The last meeting between these two sides in October saw the Red Devil's Krisnan Inu snatch a late try to lead his side to a 12-10 victory. The away side have only beaten the Saints seven times in 49 Super League meetings.
